ranges.reason: Sets up the episode's thesis: Halloween is about demons, and Christians should run toward them.
Introduces the spiritual landscape: angels, demons, and the basic conflict.
Plato's Critias quote: ancient pagan view of territorial gods.
Plato's Laws: most high god allots territories to lesser gods.
Biblical view: Deuteronomy 32:8, Babel, angels assigned to nations, fall through accepting worship.
Anthropologist Andrew Lang: transition from Most High God to lesser gods in paganism.
Galatians 4:3,9: enslaved to elemental principles (spiritual beings).
Transition: conflict between false gods and God needs resolution.
Psalm 82: God judges the gods, death of the gods.
Psalm 82 is about the whole Bible; Christ's resurrection is the death of the gods.
Isaiah 24:21, Psalm 68: enemies are fallen angels, not humans.
Job openings: humans replace fallen angels.
Saints (holy ones) terminology applied to humans in New Testament.
24 elders in Revelation: humans replace fallen angels.
Baptism as recruitment into an army; exorcism and spiritual warfare.
All Saints as a military parade; Halloween's Christian purpose.
Christ's victory is D-Day; gospel is report of military victory; recruiting for final push.
Caller Brett: recovering from paganism; baptism as liberation and change of allegiance.
Krampusnacht: Christian feast mocking and defeating demons; St. Nicholas enslaves Krampus.
Halloween customs: mocking death and demons; Orthodox All Saints hymn celebrates defeat of idols.
Practical advice: house blessing with holy water as exorcism; every house is haunted.
Early Christians saw feasts as little Passovers; ritual participation in defeat of demons.
Caller Anthony: demon in Tobit; demonic relationships are literal slavery; Christ sets free.
Caller Linda: St. Demetrius replaces pagan spirit; saints defeat demons, not adapt paganism.
Closing summary: replacement of pagan gods by saints is a historical event; saints govern with God.
Final exhortation: use holy water, sign of the cross, prayer; Christians are soldiers in a war.
Opening reading, intro music, host introductions, call-in info.
Banter about new listeners and orientation; restatement of previous episodes.
Long orientation summary of the show's purpose; already covered in universe_state.
Discussion of Halloween's various meanings; not advancing the argument.
Transition and setup; redundant.
Extended commentary on Plato quote; tangential and repetitive.
Transition and setup for anthropologist quote.
Discussion of morality and paganism; tangential.
Sound issues, break, sponsor ads.
Return from break, banter about dog.
Extended discussion of pagan morality and Zeus; tangential.
Transition to modern neo-paganism; not core to argument.
Caller Jacob: demonic dreams; response includes general advice but is lengthy and partly restatement.
Commentary on Psalm 82; restatement.
Greek nerd rabbit hole on verb 'anastethi'; tangential.
Extended emphasis on enemies not being humans; restatement.
Break, sponsor ads.
Caller Myla: is Satan an angel? Brief but not advancing main argument.
Discussion of saints terminology; restatement of previous episodes.
Extended discussion of Revelation 20 and Matthew 19; tangential.
Transition to caller Brett; banter.
Further response to Brett; includes some restatement.
Transition to practical advice; minor.
Personal anecdote about trick-or-treating; not advancing argument.
Break, sponsor ads.
Return from break, banter about joy of battle.
Caller Anthony: setup and pleasantries.
Caller Anthony follow-up; tangential.
Transition to closing; minor.
Outro, credits, closing reading.
